question.text: Under Section 6(4) of SCRA, when an inquiry is undertaken, who is bound to produce books of accounts and documents before the inquiring authority?

answer.text: Only the directors of the stock exchange
Directors, managers, members, partners, and any person who had dealings with them
Only the member of the stock exchange under inquiry
Only the compliance officer of the stock exchange

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 6(4) casts a wide net requiring every director, manager, secretary or other officer of the exchange; every member; every partner, manager, secretary of the firm; and any person who had dealings directly or indirectly with them to cooperate. **The Trap:** Limiting it to only directors or only the member under inquiry ignores the broad scope of Section 6(4). **Cross-Exam Context:** Powers of inquiry and investigation are tested in NISM Series III-B and other exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even third parties who had business dealings with the entity under inquiry can be compelled to produce documents.
